channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Concerns over shift from natural deep seaport to dugout port at Ibaka
item.description: Di Ibaka Deep Seaport for Akwa Ibom don dey planned since over 20 years back as one of di biggest maritime infrastructure projects for Nigeria and Gulf of Guinea. Di original plan, wey former Governor...

item.title: 18-year-old allegedly assaults people at Belconnen Mall while unclothed
item.description: On Saturday evening, around 5:15pm, police responded to a shocking incident at Belconnen Mall involving an 18-year-old man who allegedly removed all his clothing and ran through the shopping centre. D...

item.title: Paddy and Christine McGuinness struggle to sell Cheshire mansion amid pricing dispute
item.description: Paddy McGuinness and his former wife Christine McGuinness are in disagreement over their seven-bedroom Cheshire property, which has been on the market for over a year with an asking price of £6.5milli...

item.title: Roy Rogers and Dale Evans: Life, Career, and Legacy of Hollywood’s Western Icons
item.description: Roy Rogers and Dale Evans became one of Hollywood’s most beloved couples, earning the titles 'King and Queen of the West' during the 1940s and 1950s. They first met in 1944 on the set of 'The Cowboy a...

item.title: Daily Feng Shui Insights from Master Hanz Cua for May 17, 2026
item.description: Master Hanz Cua, a well-known feng shui expert in the Philippines, shared his predictions for May 17, 2026. While the article does not provide detailed daily guidance, it emphasizes the importance of ...

item.title: Exploring Age, Creativity, and Collaboration Through Photography: Scott and Grace Offen's Seven-Year Project
item.description: Scott Offen and his wife, Grace, collaborated on a seven-year photography project exploring themes of ageing, gender, and creativity. Initiated in 2017 after a teacher suggested Scott photograph his f...

item.title: Stage adaptation captures the depth and emotion of Tim Winton’s The Shepherd’s Hut
item.description: Tim Winton’s 2018 novel 'The Shepherd’s Hut' has been compellingly adapted for the stage by Tim McGarry, with Matt Edgerton directing a production that fully honours its literary origins while craftin...

item.title: West Bengal to Re-verify Caste Certificates Issued Since 2011 Amid Allegations of Irregularities
item.description: The West Bengal government, now led by the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP), has issued directives to re-verify all caste certificates issued in the state since 2011, citing widespread irregularities duri...

item.title: Philippine Authorities Delay Arrest of Senator Bato dela Rosa Amid ICC Warrant Confusion
item.description: Senator Ronald 'Bato' dela Rosa, who has an outstanding International Criminal Court (ICC) warrant for crimes against humanity, resurfaced at the Senate from May 11 to 13 after six months in hiding. H...

item.title: Film Review: Guy Ritchie's 'In The Grey' Delivers Action and Style
item.description: Guy Ritchie's latest film, 'In The Grey', is a 98-minute action thriller that sticks closely to the director's usual formula of crime capers, gunfights, and stylised coolness. The plot revolves around...

item.title: Maldivian diver dies during mission to recover Italian divers from underwater cave
item.description: Maldivian authorities suspended search operations after a military diver, Mohamed Mahudhee, tragically died while attempting to recover the bodies of four Italian divers in a perilous underwater cave ...

item.title: Tobi Amusan Aims to Win Third African 100m Hurdles Gold in Accra
item.description: World record holder and five-time national champion Tobi Amusan dey ready to chase her third African 100 metres hurdles crown as she kicks off her campaign at the 2026 African Senior Athletics Champio...

item.title: Michael Caine’s Career and Iconic Roles in the 1970s
item.description: Sir Michael Caine, born Maurice Joseph Micklewhite on March 14, 1933, is a legendary English actor recognized for his distinctive Cockney accent and extensive filmography spanning over 130 movies acro...

item.title: Trump-Backed Candidate Julia Letlow Advances to Runoff After Cassidy Loses Louisiana GOP Primary
item.description: In Louisiana's recent Republican primary, Sen. Bill Cassidy, a six-year incumbent who voted to convict former President Donald Trump during his first impeachment trial, lost his bid for renomination. ...

item.title: Xabi Alonso Near Agreement To Manage Chelsea After Recent Real Madrid Exit
item.description: Chelsea dey prepare to announce Xabi Alonso as their new manager, just four months after dem sack am for Real Madrid. According to media reports, Alonso don reach agreement in principle with Chelsea a...

item.title: PTI Leadership Affirms Unity Amid Internal Differences
item.description: Islamabad – Pakistan Tehreek-i-Insaf (PTI) has emphasised that its leadership, parliamentary members, and party workers remain united behind founding chairman Imran Khan despite reports of internal di...

item.title: Nonmotorized Soapbox Carts Race Through Downtown Los Angeles
item.description: On Saturday, downtown Los Angeles hosted the Red Bull Soapbox Race, a whimsical event featuring nonmotorized carts racing down 1st Street. Forty teams from across the country competed, with carts crea...

item.title: Exploring Multi-Sensory Architecture for Inclusive Public Spaces
item.description: Erin Naidoo, a recent master’s graduate from the University of KwaZulu-Natal, conducted research challenging the traditional vision-focused approach in architecture. Her study focused on how visually ...

item.title: Man in his 30s arrested after car rams into pedestrians in Modena, injuring eight
item.description: Eight people were injured, two of them seriously, after a man in his 30s drove a car into a group of pedestrians in the northern Italian city of Modena on Saturday. The driver, an Italian national of ...

item.title: Execution of William Kemmler: The First Use of the Electric Chair in 1890
item.description: William Kemmler (1860–1890) was an American produce merchant who became the first person in history executed by electric chair. In March 1889, Kemmler murdered his common-law wife, Matilda Ziegler, du...
